prompts: Please describe why you are interested in studying at Princeton. (250 words)
Briefly elaborate on an activity, organization, work experience, or hobby that has been particularly meaningful to you. (150 words)
At Princeton, we value diverse perspectives. Tell us about a person who has influenced you in a significant way. (250 words)
Princeton has a longstanding commitment to service. How does your interest in service shape your identity? (250 words)
What song represents the soundtrack of your life at this moment? (50 words)
What field of study appeals to you the most right now, and why? (100 words)
We know you lead a busy life, full of activities. Tell us about something you do simply for the pleasure of it. (200 words)
How has the world you come from shaped who you are today? (200 words)
Tell us about a significant challenge you have faced or an important issue that concerns you. (200 words)
Describe the world you come from and how it has shaped your dreams and aspirations. (200 words)
Harvard has long recognized the importance of enrolling a diverse student body. How will the life experiences that shape who you are today enable you to contribute to Harvard? (200 words)
Briefly describe an intellectual experience that was important to you. (200 words)
Briefly describe an experience where you had to navigate a significant disagreement or difference in opinion. (200 words)
How do you hope to use your Harvard education in the future? (200 words)
What would you want your future college roommate to know about you? (200 words)
The Stanford community is deeply curious and driven to learn in and out of the classroom. Reflect on an idea or experience that makes you genuinely excited about learning. (250 words)
Virtually all of Stanford's undergraduates live on campus. Write a note to your future roommate that reveals something about you. (250 words)
Tell us about something that is meaningful to you, and why? (250 words)
What is the most significant challenge that society faces today? (50 words)
How did you spend your last two summers? (50 words each)

Students at Yale have time to explore their academic interests before committing to one or more major fields of study. Many students find that their academic interests change as they learn more. Tell us about a topic or idea that excites you. (200 words)
What is it about Yale that has led you to apply? (125 words)
Short Takes: What inspires you? / If you could teach a college course, what would it be? / What is something about you that is not in your application? (35 words each)

Choose one of the following: Reflect on a time you discussed an issue important to you with someone holding an opposing view / Reflect on membership in a community to which you feel connected / Reflect on an element of your personal experience that you feel is not captured elsewhere in your application (400 words)
How did you discover your intellectual and academic interests, and how will you explore them at the University of Pennsylvania? (200 words)
At Penn, learning and growth happen outside of the classroom, too. How will you explore the community at Penn? (200 words)
What is your sense of Duke as a university and a community, and why do you consider it a good match for you? (250 words)
We want to emphasize that the following is optional. If you'd like to share a perspective you bring or experiences you've had that would help us understand you better. (250 words)
Brown's Open Curriculum allows students to explore broadly while also diving deeply into their academic pursuits. Tell us about an academic interest that excites you. (200 words)
Students entering Brown often find that the Open Curriculum inspires them in unexpected ways. Tell us about a time you were surprised by an idea or perspective. (200 words)
Brown's culture fosters collaboration and mutual respect. Tell us about a time you learned from someone with a different perspective than your own. (200 words)

Founded in the spirit of exploration and discovery, Johns Hopkins University encourages students to share their perspectives, develop their interests, and pursue new experiences. Use this space to share something you'd like the admissions committee to know about you. (400 words)

We want to better understand you. Which of these questions best suits you? Other parts of your application give us a sense of who you are; we'd now like to get a sense of your intellectual curiosity. (300 words)
List the titles of the books, essays, poetry, short stories or plays you read for pleasure that you enjoyed most in the past year. (100 words)
List the titles of the films, concerts, exhibits, or other entertainment that you enjoyed most in the past year. (100 words)
List the publications and websites you read regularly. (100 words)
Why are you interested in attending Columbia University? (200 words)
Please tell us what from your current and past experiences (either academic or personal) motivates you to study this field of study. (200 words)
Students in Arts and Sciences embrace the opportunity to delve into multifaceted academic interests. Tell us about the areas of study you are excited to explore. (250 words)
At Cornell, students are encouraged to explore and connect across disciplines. Tell us about two or three of your intellectual interests and why you are drawn to them. (250 words)
As you seek admission to Dartmouth's Class of 2030, what aspects of the college's academic program, community, or campus environment attract your interest? (100 words)
Choose one: Labor omnia vincit (Hard work conquers all) / A rising tide lifts all boats / So we beat on, boats against the current (250 words)

How does the University of Chicago, as you know it now, satisfy your desire for a particular kind of learning, community, and future? (Uncommon Essay — choose one of UChicago's unique prompts + Why UChicago)
Please explain why you wish to study in the academic areas you selected. (150 words)
Based upon your exploration of Rice University, what elements of the Rice experience appeal to you? (150 words)
Vanderbilt offers a community where your experiences and perspectives are valued. Please share how a community has shaped your perspective or identity. (250 words)
What excites you about the University of Notre Dame that makes it stand out from other institutions? (200 words)
Tell us about something that really sparks your intellectual curiosity and has made you want to learn more. (200 words)
As Georgetown is a diverse community, the Admission Committee would like to know more about you in your own words. Please submit a brief essay, either personal or creative. (1 page)
Indicate any special talents or skills you possess. (250 words)

Most students choose their intended major or area of study based on a passion or inspiration that's Search Search Search Search Search Search Search Search developed over time. What factors have influenced your decision? (300 words)
What academic areas are you interested in exploring at Emory University and why? (200 words)
What is something you have done or would like to do that connects you to the Emory community? (150 words)
Describe an example of your leadership experience in which you have positively influenced others, helped resolve disputes, or contributed to group efforts over time. (350 words)
Every person has a creative side, and it can be expressed in many ways. Describe how you express your creative side. (350 words)
What would you say is your greatest talent or skill? How have you developed and demonstrated that talent over time? (350 words)
Describe how you have taken advantage of a significant educational opportunity or worked to overcome an educational barrier you have faced. (350 words)
We would like to know more about your interest in NYU. What motivated you to apply to NYU? (400 words)
Everyone belongs to many different communities and/or groups. Choose one of the communities to which you belong, and describe that community and your place within it. (300 words)
Describe the unique qualities that attract you to the specific undergraduate College or School to which you are applying. (550 words)
Describe an example of your leadership experience in which you have positively influenced others, helped resolve disputes, or contributed to group efforts over time. (350 words)
Every person has a creative side, and it can be expressed in many ways. Describe how you express your creative side. (350 words)
What would you say is your greatest talent or skill? How have you developed and demonstrated that talent over time? (350 words)
Describe how you have taken advantage of a significant educational opportunity or worked to overcome an educational barrier you have faced. (350 words)
Which aspects of the Tufts undergraduate experience prompt your application? (150 words)
Now we'd like to know a little bit more about you. Please respond to one of the following three prompts. (200-250 words)
Describe how you plan to pursue your academic interests and why you want to explore them at USC specifically. (250 words)
Describe yourself in three words and explain why you chose them. (250 words)
Why do you want to study your chosen major at Georgia Tech? (300 words)
Georgia Tech's motto is Progress and Service. How do you intend to make a positive impact on society? (300 words)

tp: 무료 등록금. 근로 필수. 저소득층 대상.
플로리다 HBCU. 메리 맥레오드 베순 설립.
Hard Work U. 무료 등록금. 근로 필수.
뉴욕 패션공대. SUNY 시스템. 가성비 최고.
샌디에이고 비전통적 대학. 매달 수업 시작.
롤리 HBCU. 미국 남부 최초 흑인 대학 중 하나.
여대. 공학 프로그램 있는 유일 여자대학.
미국 최대 대학 중 하나. 게임디자인.
미 육군사관학교. 무료 학비. 5년 복무 의무.
텍사스 동부 HBCU. The Great Debaters.
